text.title: Junior AI-Overreliance Coaching Guide Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules for catching and coaching AI-overreliance without shaming.

rule.statement: Reviewer MUST check the PR for the 6 overreliance signals: mismatched naming, library-of-the-day, undefended exception flow, copy-pasted prompt artifacts, no-comment integration, style-mix.
rule.rationale: A named pattern library makes overreliance objective; 'feels AI-generated' is unactionable.

rule.statement: Review comments MUST be phrased as questions ("what happens if X is null?") not assertions ("this is wrong"). The junior produces the answer.
rule.rationale: Questions force re-engagement with the code; assertions invite reflex-accept.

rule.statement: Use the 4-step ladder: acknowledge (correct parts) → ask (probe one signal) → block (request changes for >=2 unaddressed signals) → escalate (loop in manager only after 3 blocked PRs).
rule.rationale: Premature escalation damages psychological safety; immediate block on first signal kills willingness to use AI.

rule.statement: Senior MUST ask the junior 'what would you write without the AI suggestion?' for at least one section per PR.
rule.rationale: Forces meta-cognition; reveals whether the junior understands the suggestion or merely accepted it.

rule.statement: Senior MUST NOT silently rewrite the junior's AI-overrelied code. Either request changes with rationale, or accept + leave a teaching comment.
rule.rationale: Silent rewrites teach nothing; the junior repeats the pattern. Visible rationale is the artefact.
